task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into Oscar EMR.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Oscar EMR main dashboard, schedule screen, or top navigation bar is visible, indicating successful login (often showing the user 'oscardoc').

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the Appointment Status configuration page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A settings, administration, or preferences page specifically for managing 'Appointment Statuses' is open and visible in the browser.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct Status Code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The letter 'V' is typed into a 'Status', 'Code', or similar input field for the new appointment status.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Vitals Done' is typed into a 'Description' or 'Meaning' input field for the new appointment status.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected a green color for the status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A green color is selected from a color picker, color wheel, or dropdown menu associated with the new appointment status.

task_completion.description: Verify the new appointment status was successfully saved and appears in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The newly created status 'V' with the description 'Vitals Done' and a green color indicator is visible in the saved list or table of existing appointment statuses.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Oscar EMR web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is interacting with a web browser showing the Oscar EMR application, rather than using a terminal, API testing tool, or script.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the GUI using database commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing SQL commands (e.g., INSERT INTO appointment_status) being executed to create the status directly in the database.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the genuine application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show progressive navigation through the Oscar EMR menus and forms, not just a text editor or static mockups.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must log into Oscar EMR using username 'oscardoc', password 'oscar', and PIN '1117'. The target appointment status code is 'V' and the description is 'Vitals Done' (case-insensitive). The selected color must be a shade of green (from the Oscar color wheel). Verify that the status is created in the actual Oscar EMR web interface.
